name: Bastille Day
description: French National Day celebrating the storming of the Bastille.
history: Commemorates the 1789 turning point of the French Revolution.
traditions: Military parades, fireworks, and balls.

name: Independence Day
description: A federal holiday in the US commemorating the Declaration of Independence.
history: Ratified on July 4, 1776, establishing the United States.
traditions: Fireworks, family barbecues, parades.

name: Canada Day
description: National day of Canada.
history: Celebrates the anniversary of the Canadian Confederation in 1867.
traditions: Parades, carnivals, festivals, fireworks.

name: Ramadan
description: The ninth month of the Islamic calendar, observed with fasting and prayer.
history: Commemorates the first revelation of the Quran to Muhammad.
traditions: Fasting from dawn to sunset, charity, nightly prayers.

name: Eid al-Fitr
description: The 'Festival of Breaking the Fast' marking the end of Ramadan.
history: Originated by the Islamic prophet Muhammad.
traditions: Special prayers, feasting, giving Zakat al-Fitr.

name: Eid al-Adha
description: The 'Feast of the Sacrifice'.
history: Honors Ibrahim's willingness to sacrifice his son.
traditions: Sacrificing livestock, sharing meat with the poor.

name: Easter
description: Christian holiday celebrating the resurrection of Jesus.
history: The culmination of the Passion of Jesus.
traditions: Church services, Easter egg hunts, festive family meals.

name: Christmas
description: Annual festival commemorating the birth of Jesus Christ.
history: Originated as a Christian feast blending with winter solstice traditions.
traditions: Gift-giving, Christmas trees, church services.

name: Yom Kippur
description: The holiest day of the year in Judaism.
history: Described in the Torah as a day of cleansing.
traditions: 25-hour fasting, intensive prayer, synagogue services.

name: Hanukkah
description: The Jewish Festival of Lights.
history: Commemorates the rededication of the Second Temple in Jerusalem.
traditions: Lighting the menorah, playing dreidel, eating fried foods.

name: Lollapalooza
description: Annual four-day music festival held in Chicago.
history: Started as a touring festival in 1991 by Perry Farrell.
traditions: Alternative rock, heavy metal, punk rock, hip hop, and EDM performances.

name: Tomorrowland Winter
description: Winter edition of the famous electronic music festival.
history: First held in 2019 in the French Alps.
traditions: Skiing, electronic music stages on snowy mountains.

name: South by Southwest
description: Annual conglomeration of parallel film, interactive media, and music festivals.
history: Began in 1987 in Austin, Texas.
traditions: Tech panels, film premieres, live music showcases.

name: Venice Film Festival
description: The world's oldest film festival.
history: Founded in 1932 by Giuseppe Volpi.
traditions: Awarding the Golden Lion, glamorous red carpets.

name: Berlin International Film Festival
description: One of the world's leading film festivals.
history: Founded in West Berlin in 1951.
traditions: Awarding the Golden Bear and Silver Bear.

name: Day of the Dead (Oaxaca)
description: One of Mexico's most vibrant and traditional Dia de los Muertos celebrations.
history: Ancient indigenous traditions blended with colonial influences.
traditions: Elaborate sand tapestries, comparsas (parades), night vigils.

name: Gion Matsuri
description: One of Japan's most famous festivals held in Kyoto.
history: Originated in 869 as a religious purification ritual.
traditions: Massive Yamaboko floats, traditional street food, yukata wearing.

name: Festa delle Marie
description: A traditional parade during the Venice Carnival.
history: Commemorates the rescue of twelve Venetian brides in the 10th century.
traditions: Historical costume parade, crowning the 'Maria of the Year'.

name: Timkat
description: Ethiopian Orthodox celebration of Epiphany.
history: An ancient Christian tradition unique to the Ethiopian church.
traditions: Procession of the Tabot, ritual baptism, singing and dancing.

name: Trinidad and Tobago Carnival
description: One of the most famous Caribbean carnivals.
history: Originated from French planters' masquerade balls in the 18th century.
traditions: Calypso music, steelpan competitions, spectacular masquerade bands.

name: Notting Hill Carnival
description: Annual Caribbean carnival event taking place in London.
history: Started in 1966 to celebrate Afro-Caribbean communities.
traditions: Sound systems, vibrant costumes, Caribbean street food.

name: Vesak (Buddha Day)
description: Holiday observed traditionally by Buddhists.
history: Commemorates the birth, enlightenment, and death of Gautama Buddha.
traditions: Lighting lanterns, vegetarian food, visiting temples.

name: Yule
description: Historical winter festival celebrated by Germanic peoples.
history: Pre-Christian indigenous winter solstice festival.
traditions: Burning the Yule log, feasting, singing.

name: Cinco de Mayo
description: Annual celebration honoring a Mexican military victory.
history: Commemorates the Mexican Army's victory over the French at the Battle of Puebla in 1862.
traditions: Parades, mariachi music, traditional foods.

name: Oktoberfest Blumenau
description: The largest German festival in the Americas.
history: Started in 1984 to boost morale after a major flood.
traditions: German beer, traditional dancing, Bavarian cuisine.

name: St. Lucia's Day
description: Christian feast day widely celebrated in Scandinavia.
history: Honors Lucy of Syracuse, an early-4th-century virgin martyr.
traditions: Girls wearing white dresses and candle wreaths, singing songs.

name: Walpurgis Night
description: Spring festival marking the eve of the Christian feast day of Saint Walpurga.
history: Associated with witches gathering on the Brocken mountain.
traditions: Lighting bonfires, dressing in costumes, dancing.

name: Midsummer
description: Celebration of the summer solstice.
history: Ancient pagan tradition celebrating fertility and light.
traditions: Dancing around the maypole, wearing flower crowns.

name: Barranquilla's Carnival
description: One of Colombia's most important folkloric celebrations.
history: A blend of European, African, and Indigenous traditions dating back centuries.
traditions: Cumbia dancing, Battle of Flowers parade, colorful costumes.

name: International Kite Festival
description: Marks the days in the Hindu calendar when winter begins turning to summer.
history: Celebrated since 1989 in Ahmedabad.
traditions: Flying thousands of kites, kite-fighting competitions.

name: Bikaner Camel Festival
description: Celebration of the ship of the desert, the camel.
history: Organized by the Department of Tourism, Rajasthan.
traditions: Camel races, camel dancing, fur shearing competitions.

name: Feria de las Flores
description: The most important social event for the city of Medellín.
history: Started in 1957 to celebrate the region's flower industry.
traditions: Silleteros parade (flower carriers), classic car parades.

name: Galway Oyster Festival
description: The world's longest-running oyster festival.
history: Established in 1954.
traditions: Oyster opening championships, seafood tasting.

name: Winterlude
description: Annual winter festival in the capital region of Canada.
history: Created in 1979 to celebrate Canada's winter climate and culture.
traditions: Ice skating on the Rideau Canal, ice sculptures.

name: Calgary Stampede
description: Annual rodeo, exhibition, and festival.
history: Evolved from local agricultural fairs dating back to 1886.
traditions: Rodeo events, chuckwagon races, pancake breakfasts.

name: Rio de Janeiro New Year's Eve
description: Massive New Year's Eve party on Copacabana beach.
history: One of the largest NYE parties in the world.
traditions: Wearing white for luck, jumping 7 waves, massive fireworks.

name: Lathmar Holi
description: A unique local celebration of Holi in Barsana and Nandgaon.
history: Based on a legend of Lord Krishna visiting Radha's village.
traditions: Women chasing men with wooden sticks (lathis) in a playful manner.

name: Taiwan International Balloon Festival
description: Major hot air balloon event in Taitung.
history: Started in 2011 to boost local tourism.
traditions: Night glow concerts, hot air balloon flights.

name: Awa Odori
description: The largest dance festival in Japan.
history: Has a history of over 400 years originating in Tokushima.
traditions: Groups of choreographed dancers (ren) parading through streets.

name: Ati-Atihan Festival
description: Philippine festival held annually in honor of the Santo Niño.
history: Considered the Mother of All Philippine Festivals.
traditions: Tribal dance, music, indigenous costumes and weapons.

name: Sinulog Festival
description: A cultural and religious festival held in Cebu City.
history: Commemorates the Filipino people's pagan origin and their acceptance of Roman Catholicism.
traditions: Grand street parade with traditional Sinulog dance.

name: MassKara Festival
description: The 'Festival of Smiles' in Bacolod.
history: Started in 1980 during a period of crisis to lift the spirits of the locals.
traditions: Dancers wearing smiling masks with intricate details.

name: Kandy Esala Perahera
description: Grand Buddhist festival featuring decorated elephants.
history: Dates back to the 3rd century BC.
traditions: Fire dances, whip-dances, Kandyan dances, elephant processions.

name: Puli Kali
description: Recreational folk art from Kerala, India.
history: Introduced by Maharaja Rama Varma Sakthan Thampuran 200 years ago.
traditions: Performers painted like tigers and hunters dancing to the beats of instruments.

name: Chinchilla Melon Festival
description: A quirky festival celebrating the town's melon farming.
history: Started in 1994 to lift the town's spirits after a severe drought.
traditions: Melon skiing, melon bungee, melon tossing.

name: Dark Mofo
description: Winter festival run by the Museum of Old and New Art.
history: Launched in 2013 to celebrate the darkness of the southern winter solstice.
traditions: Nude solstice swim, large-scale public art, winter feasts.

name: Waitangi Day
description: The national day of New Zealand.
history: Commemorates the signing of the Treaty of Waitangi in 1840.
traditions: Māori cultural performances, speeches, community festivals.

name: Matariki
description: Māori New Year festival.
history: Based on the rising of the Pleiades star cluster.
traditions: Stargazing, remembering the dead, feasting.

name: Pasifika Festival
description: A Pacific Islands-themed festival in Auckland.
history: Started in 1993 to celebrate the city's Pacific Island communities.
traditions: Traditional Polynesian dancing, food stalls, crafts.
